sections.summary: Defines the scope as applying to dried shark fins intended for further processing.

Describes the product and process: defines dried shark fins, requires a drying process to meet Section 3.2.4, and sets permitted presentations (e.g., skin‑on, skinless) with labelling requirements to avoid consumer confusion.
Defines dried shark fins as dorsal and pectoral fins cut in an arc and the lower lobe of the caudal fin cut straight, with all flesh removed, from shark species safe for human consumption.
Requires that fins be subjected to a drying process so as to meet the requirements of Section 3.2.4 and comply with the conditions laid down in the standard.

Permits presentation of dried shark fins with skin on or skinless and allows other presentations provided they meet all standard requirements and are adequately described on the label to avoid misleading consumers.

sections.section: 2.3.1 Dried shark fins may be presented with the skin on or as skinless
sections.summary: Permits dried shark fins to be presented either with the skin on or as skinless.
Allows other forms of presentation only if they meet all other requirements of the standard and are adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

Sets essential composition and quality factors: fins must come from sound sharks fit for fresh sale and no other ingredients are permitted, and the final product must be free from foreign material, objectionable odours, and objectionable texture; the final product shall have a moisture content not exceeding 18%.
Requires dried shark fins to be prepared from sound sharks of a quality fit to be sold fresh for human consumption.
States that no other ingredients are permitted in the product.

Specifies final product requirements: the product must be free from foreign material, objectionable odours, and objectionable textural characteristics, and must have a moisture content not exceeding 18%.
Requires the final product to be free from foreign material.
Requires the product to be free from objectionable odours.
Requires dried shark fins to be free from objectionable textural characteristics.
Sets a moisture limit: the final product shall have a moisture content not exceeding 18%.
Document title only: 'STANDARD FOR DRIED SHARK FINS'.
Document code only: 'CODEX STAN 189-1993'.
Prohibits any food additives in the product; no additives are permitted.

Requires the final product to be free from any foreign material that poses a health threat and, when tested by Codex-prescribed sampling and examination methods, to be free from microorganisms or microbial substances and any other substances in amounts that may present a health hazard; recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the Codex General Principles of Food Hygiene and the Recommended International Code of Practice for Fresh Fish.

Sets labelling requirements: the name must be “dried shark fins” or an appropriate local name with a clear reference to the form of presentation, and the label must also include species, fin type and size; for non-retail containers required information (product name, lot identification and manufacturer/packer name and address) may appear on the container or accompanying documents and may be replaced by an identifiable mark linked to those documents.

Mandates that samples for sensory and physical examination be assessed by trained personnel following the procedures in Section 7.3, Annex B (“Sensoric and Physical Examination”), and the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CAC/GL 31-1999).
Requires determination of the net weight (exclusive of packaging) for each sample unit in the sample lot.
Method for determination of moisture is not yet developed (placeholder).
Defines a defective sample unit as one that fails to meet the final product requirements referenced in Section 3.3.

Defines foreign matter as any material not derived from fish that is readily recognized (without magnification) or detectable by methods including magnification and whose presence indicates non-compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices, even if it does not pose a health threat.
Defines an odour defect as a sample unit affected by persistent, distinct objectionable odours indicative of decomposition.
Defines a texture defect as textural breakdown of the fin (softness) indicative of decomposition.
Specifies a moisture defect where the sample unit exceeds 18% moisture.

Specifies lot acceptance criteria: total defectives (per Section 8) must not exceed the acceptance number (c) of a sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5; the average net weight of sampled units must be at least the declared weight with no unreasonable shortage in any container; presentation non-conformities must not exceed the acceptance number (c) with an AQL of 6.5; and the Food Additive, Hygiene and Handling, and Labelling requirements of Sections 4, 5.1, 5.2 and 6 must be met.
Non-substantive annex heading containing an image placeholder (Annex A); no substantive text provided.
